sanskrit:
आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेनमाश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः।
आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः शृणोति श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित्॥
transliteration: āścaryavat paśyati kaścid enam āścaryavad vadati tathaiva cānyaḥ | āścaryavac cainam anyaḥ śṛṇoti śrutvāpy enaṃ veda na caiva kaścit ||
literal_meaning: Someone sees this (self) as a wonder; someone speaks of it as a wonder; another hears of it as a wonder; yet, having heard, no one actually knows it.
contextual_meaning: The self is so subtle that most people only glimpse, talk about, or hear rumors of its existence; direct embodied knowledge is rare and requires sustained attention beyond intellectual curiosity.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance between conceptual fascination and lived realization; the mind circles the self like a moth around a flame without ever landing.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling past yet another viral post on 'finding your true self'—you bookmark it, discuss it at brunch, but still feel hollow because insight hasn't moved from feed to flesh.
anchor_text: The self is felt, not figured out; keep quiet and feel it.
